title: GOOD: cfg-gate wasm32-incompatible deps in nie-core
description: nie-core gates tokio, tungstenite, rpassword, openmls, and transport/keyfile/mls modules under cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32")) in both Cargo.toml and lib.rs. This lets nie-wasm reuse nie-core (identity, auth, messages, protocol) directly without a separate crate split or feature flags. The alternative was a nie-core-wasm shim crate or duplicating the shared types — both would create drift between web and native implementations of the wire protocol and identity primitives. The cfg-gate approach keeps a single source of truth for all shared types while allowing the wasm32 build to omit the std::fs, tokio, and native-TLS transitive deps that cannot compile to wasm32.
